page: Microsoft Azure M64 is a memory-optimized server belonging to the MS family, powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8280M processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor runs at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz and includes 38 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core, and includes 7340 GB of local storage. No GPUs are bundled with this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, as evidenced by bottom-tier results in stress-ng single-core CPU tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core and memory-bound operations perform significantly better. The server achieves strong results in large-block read-only memory bandwidth (216.37 GB/sec) and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. General multi-core processing, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation fall within average ranges.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the M64 server offers high resource density for memory-heavy workloads, though its lower single-core performance represents a trade-off for compute-bound tasks. The massive 1 TB memory capacity and substantial 7340 GB local storage make this instance appropriate for large-scale database hosting, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and multi-threaded data processing pipelines where massive RAM allocation is prioritized over high single-thread clock speeds.
description: Microsoft Azure M64 is a memory-optimized server from the MS family, featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8280M processor running at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it delivers 1024.0 GB of RAM alongside 7340 GB of local storage, with no integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show weak single-core execution, but strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and large-block read-only memory bandwidth. Multi-core CPU tasks, database operations, and static web serving perform in the average range. Qualitatively, the server provides high memory density, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads despite its lower single-core efficiency. It is best suited for large databases, enterprise applications, and memory-heavy data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M64 is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1024 GB of RAM, and 7340 GB of local storage, designed for large-scale database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M64 is a memory-optimized server with 64 Intel vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 7340 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized configuration featuring one terabyte of RAM and substantial local storage for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1024 GB of RAM
7340 GB of local storage included without GPU accelerators
Strong multi-threaded decompression and large-block read memory bandwidth
Weak single-core performance offset by high memory density
Suited for large databases and memory-intensive enterprise applications
